name: typer
what_it_does: Modern CLI framework for Python using type hints — builds CLI tools using Python type annotations instead of decorators. typer features: app = typer.Typer() with @app.command(), function type hints auto-generate CLI arguments/options, Optional[str] creates optional parameter, Annotated for rich help text, typer.Argument() and typer.Option() for detailed configs, automatic --help generation, rich-powered output (panels, tables), progress bars, color, confirmation prompts, testing via typer.testing.CliRunner, and sub-applications for command groups. Built on click by FastAPI author.
use_cases: Agent type-hint CLI — import typer; app = typer.Typer(); @app.command(); def process(name: str, count: int = 1, verbose: bool = False): typer.echo(f'Processing {name} {count} times'); if __name__ == '__main__': app() — type-hint CLI; agent creates CLI using type annotations; str → required argument; int with default → optional with default; bool → --verbose/--no-verbose flag

Agent annotated options — from typing import Annotated; import typer; @app.command(); def create(name: Annotated[str, typer.Argument(help='Name to create')], output: Annotated[str, typer.Option('--output', '-o', help='Output path')] = '.') — rich metadata; agent adds help text, short options, and rich formatting via Annotated

Agent sub-commands — app = typer.Typer(); users_app = typer.Typer(); app.add_typer(users_app, name='users'); @users_app.command('create'); def create_user(name: str): ...; @users_app.command('delete'); def delete_user(name: str): ... — nested commands; agent builds complex CLIs with command hierarchies like 'mytool users create'

Agent enum choices — from enum import Enum; class Color(str, Enum): red = 'red'; green = 'green'; @app.command(); def paint(color: Color): typer.echo(f'Painting {color.value}') — type-safe choices; agent restricts argument to valid enum values; typer auto-generates --help with valid choices listed

Agent rich progress — import typer; from rich.progress import track; @app.command(); def process(items: list[str]): for item in track(items, description='Processing...'): work(item) — rich progress; agent combines typer CLI with rich output; typer installs rich as optional dependency for enhanced output
not_for: Complex argument customization — typer is simpler than click; for fine-grained control over argument behavior use click directly
Non-Python-3.9+ projects — typer uses modern type hints; for Python 3.6 compatibility use click
TUI applications — typer is for CLI argument parsing; for interactive TUI use textual/urwid
best_when: Building Python CLI tools with type safety — typer is ideal when type hints are already used in the codebase and FastAPI-style patterns are preferred over click decorators.
avoid_when: Fine-grained click control needed, Python 3.6 compatibility required, TUI applications, or when the team is already familiar with click.

pricing.notes: typer is MIT licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. Performance: negligible argument parsing overhead. rich import adds ~50ms startup if enabled. For fast CLI startup: typer.Typer(no_args_is_help=True, add_completion=False) skips some initialization.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: click.BadParameter for type validation failures — inherited from click. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=bad argument. rich-powered error output when rich is installed. Error messages include option name and expected type.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CLI commands are per-execution. Argument parsing is deterministic. typer.testing.CliRunner invocations are isolated.
known_agent_gotchas: bool options generate --flag/--no-flag pair — def cmd(verbose: bool = False) generates --verbose AND --no-verbose; click generates only --verbose; agent code: use typer.Option(is_flag=True) for single-flag behavior; or Annotated[bool, typer.Option('--verbose/--no-verbose')] for explicit pair

Optional[str] is None by default, not required — def cmd(name: Optional[str] = None) creates optional --name that defaults to None; to make string required: def cmd(name: str) without default; agent code often makes the mistake of typing Optional[str] expecting required-but-optional, which typer makes completely optional

List arguments use list type hint — def cmd(names: list[str]) accepts repeated positional args: names= ['a', 'b', 'c']; for options: Optional[list[str]] = None; typer 0.9+ uses list[str] not List[str]; agent code on Python 3.8: use List[str] from typing

app() call required for entry point — typer.Typer() creates app; @app.command() registers command; app() in __main__ block is required to run; without app() call, importing module has no effect; agent setup.py/pyproject.toml entry_points: point to module_name:app not the decorated function

typer.echo vs print — typer.echo() is preferred and captures to typer.testing.CliRunner; print() is not captured in tests; agent code mixing print() and typer.echo() will have test inconsistencies; typer.secho(text, fg='green') for colored output; typer.style() for inline styling

Annotated requires Python 3.9+ or from __future__ import annotations — Annotated[str, typer.Argument()] syntax requires Python 3.9+; for Python 3.7-3.8: from __future__ import annotations at top of file enables it; or use click.argument()/option() style without Annotated; agent code targeting Python 3.7 must handle this

security_score_components.security_notes: CLI framework built on click with no network calls. Type hint validation prevents some injection attacks. Password inputs via typer.Option(prompt=True, hide_input=True). Do not log CLI arguments that may contain secrets — check help text descriptions.
